title: Final QA + release v1.0.0: runtime matrix, drift check, screenshot, Pages, tag
description:
FINAL QA + RELEASE v1.0.0 - orchestrator-owned (claude-fable), not dispatched to workers.
CHECKLIST:
- Full gate suite green on main (node --test, node build.mjs).
- Runtime matrix: live mode (python http.server), snapshot mode (file:// + refresh output), demo mode (bare file), Edge/Chromium, dark + light + reduced-motion, narrow viewport (~380px).
- Real-world wiring test: scripts/install.ps1 into a scratch clone of another project with a real .beads; panel renders it with zero curation.
- Adversarial review completed and clean on bmc-5 + bmc-7 (0.10 mandatory) - VERIFIED ledger lines present on both.
- Drift check (0.12): every closed bmc-* bead has a result=pass ledger line.
- Screenshot: capture panel over this repo's data -> docs/screenshot.png, referenced by README.
- Enable GitHub Pages (gh api, /docs on main), verify demo URL is live.
- Tag v1.0.0 + GitHub release with summary; bump package.json to 1.0.0.

title: Panel behaviors: data chain live/snapshot/demo, filters, search, config, theme toggle
description:
CONTEXT: engine (bmc-2) + theme/markup contract (bmc-3) done. You own src/panel.js. Wire everything into a living panel.
BEHAVIORS:
- init(): resolve data via chain: (1) fetch(config.dataPath || "../.beads/issues.jsonl", cache no-store) -> parseJSONL; (2) on failure window.BMC_SNAPSHOT (from optional orchestration-data.js, shape {generated_at, issues:[...]}); (3) else built-in tiny demo dataset + "no data" hint. Set #src indicator: live / snapshot (+age from generated_at) / demo.
- render(model): waves + cards per bmc-3 contract (use template#card-tpl), counts chips, arc-reactor pct, blocked-by list, assignee readout on inprogress, verification badge slot (bmc-6 fills), decision flag note from meta.
- filters: status chips (all/ready/inprogress/blocked/done/deferred), free-text search #search (id+title+track+assignee, debounced), track filter (chip row derived from data). Filters compose (AND).
- copy: click .id copies "bd show <id>" with toast.
- auto-refresh: toggle #auto, interval config.refreshInterval||15000, only re-fetches in live mode.
- theme: #themetoggle cycles auto -> dark -> light, persists localStorage key bmc-theme, stamps [data-theme] on root.
- config: read window.BMC_CONFIG {title, accent, dataPath, refreshInterval, strings, metaPath}; apply title, set --bmc-accent, deep-merge strings into all data-i18n nodes; optional meta overlay: if config.metaPath set, fetch it (JSON) and pass to deriveModel; if window.BMC_META present (file:// path), use it - both optional, failures degrade silently (console.info).
- ALL DOM lookups via the bmc-3 contract; if a hook is missing, console.warn once, never throw.
TESTS: pure helpers extracted and unit-tested (test/panel-helpers.test.mjs): filter composition, search matcher, source-resolution decision (given mocked availability flags), strings deep-merge, snapshot age formatting. DOM code stays thin and untested.
GATE: node --test && node build.mjs -> exit 0. Runtime acceptance by orchestrator: panel over fixtures via local server (live), via file:// with generated data file (snapshot), and bare (demo).
DO NOT TOUCH: src/engine.mjs internals (extend only via its public API - if you need an engine change, STOP and comment on the bead instead), src/panel.css (request additions via bead comment if a class is missing).

title: Scaffold: repo layout, zero-dep build.mjs, test harness, realistic fixture, MIT license
description:
CONTEXT: empty repo (README+.beads only). Create the full skeleton so every later bead has a build+test harness.
CREATE:
- package.json: name bd-mission-control, version 0.1.0, type module, private true, scripts {build: node build.mjs, test: node --test} - NO dependencies, ever.
- src/panel.template.html: minimal valid HTML5 skeleton with placeholders {{VERSION}}, {{CSS}}, {{ENGINE_JS}}, {{PANEL_JS}} and the top-level containers (header #hud-header with #ring, #chips; #toolbar; #waves; #legend; #foot). Keep it semantic; real markup arrives with bmc-3.
- src/panel.css: stub with :root{} custom props block only.
- src/engine.mjs: stub exporting parseJSONL() and deriveModel() (throw new Error("not implemented") bodies are fine).
- src/panel.js: stub with init() no-op.
- build.mjs: zero-dep ESM node script. Reads template, inlines CSS and both JS files at placeholders (engine must be inlined WITHOUT its export statements - strip the "export " keyword into plain declarations, document the convention in a comment), stamps {{VERSION}} from package.json. Writes dist/orchestration.html. Must fail loudly (exit 1) if any placeholder remains unresolved in output.
- test/build.test.mjs: node --test smoke: runs build via child_process, asserts dist file exists, contains version string, contains no "{{".
- fixtures/issues.sample.jsonl: synthetic but REALISTIC bd export, ~25 lines: 2 epics (issue_type epic) with dot-children (e.g. smp-a1.1), plus standalone beads; statuses open/in_progress/closed/deferred mix; assignees; labels using track:X, thinking:high|medhi|med|low, wave:N; dependencies [{depends_on_id, type:"blocks"}]; several closed issues carrying comments array where at least 2 have a comment whose text starts with "VERIFIED bead=<id> result=pass" and 1 with "VERIFICATION_FAILED". Mirror the real field names from a bd 1.1.0 export (id,title,description,status,priority,issue_type,assignee,labels,dependencies,comments,created_at,closed_at).
- LICENSE: MIT, holder "Marco Cardia (maisdesign)", year 2026.
- .gitignore: node_modules/, *.log. dist/ IS COMMITTED (vendoring channel) - do not ignore it.
DO NOT TOUCH: .beads/, CLAUDE.md, README.md.
GATE: node build.mjs && node --test -> both exit 0.

title: Data engine: JSONL parse + auto-derive waves/state/track/thinking + meta overlay + ledger
description:
CONTEXT: scaffold from bmc-1 exists. Implement the pure data engine in src/engine.mjs. No DOM, no fetch - pure functions only, fully unit-tested. This is the heart of the auto-derivation promise.
API (exported, JSDoc-typed):
- parseJSONL(text) -> issue[] : tolerant (skip blank/corrupt lines, never throw).
- deriveModel(issues, meta = {}, config = {}) -> { waves: [{key, title, subtitle, ids:[...]}], beads: {id: {state, blockedBy, phase, track, thinking, label, assignee, verification, epic, note, flag}}, counts: {all,ready,inprogress,blocked,done,deferred}, pct }
RULES (precedence: meta overlay > labels > heuristic):
- state: status closed -> done; in_progress -> inprogress; deferred (status or label "deferred") -> deferred; open with any non-closed blocker (dependencies type "blocks" or plain, resolved against issue set) -> blocked; else ready.
- epic membership: dot-id children (x.1 belongs to epic x) OR dependency type "parent-child". Each epic founds a wave (ordered by epic created_at). Standalone beads: label wave:N groups them (one wave per N, title from meta.waves[N] or "Wave N"); no label -> single "backlog" wave.
- track: meta.beads[id].track -> label track:X -> uppercase issue_type.
- thinking: meta.beads[id].thinking -> label thinking:X -> priority map {0:high, 1:medhi, 2:med, >=3:low}.
- verification (ledger): scan comments[].text; /^VERIFIED\b.*result=pass/ -> "pass"; /^VERIFICATION_FAILED\b/ -> "fail"; else null. Closed WITHOUT pass line is visually distinct later (bmc-6) - engine just reports.
- waveOrder: waves with >=1 ready|inprogress first (stable order within group), exhausted waves sink to bottom. Same rule as predecessor panel.
- meta overlay shape: {waves: {key:{title,subtitle,order}}, beads: {id:{wave,phase,track,thinking,note,flag}}} - every field optional, unknown ids ignored, missing entries synthesized (NEVER require curation).
- config.strings merge: deep-merge user strings over EN defaults; engine exposes defaultStrings.
TESTS (test/engine.test.mjs): fixture-driven against fixtures/issues.sample.jsonl + inline micro-cases: corrupt line skipped; blocked chain (A blocks B blocks C); dot-child epic grouping; label overrides heuristic; meta overrides label; deferred label; ledger pass/fail/null; counts+pct; waveOrder sinking; missing optional fields (no assignee/labels/comments) never throw.
GATE: node --test -> exit 0, all new tests green.
DO NOT TOUCH: src/panel.css, src/panel.template.html, src/panel.js (bmc-3/bmc-4 own them).

title: EPIC: bd-mission-control v1 - single-file JARVIS mission control panel for bd
description:
GOAL: reusable, single-file, JARVIS/Stark-HUD styled mission control panel for beads (bd) orchestration, published as public repo github.com/maisdesign/bd-mission-control (MIT).

ARCHITECTURE (decided with Marco 2026-07-11):
- Distribution: ONE vendored file dist/orchestration.html (committed build artifact). Per-project wiring = 3 optional sibling files: orchestration.config.js (window.BMC_CONFIG), orchestration-data.js (window.BMC_SNAPSHOT, generated), refresh scripts.
- Structure is AUTO-DERIVED from .beads/issues.jsonl (waves/track/thinking from labels+heuristics); optional meta overlay enriches, never required. This kills the hand-curated META maintenance burden of the predecessor (orizzontediploma/docs/orchestration.html).
- Data resolution chain in panel: 1) live fetch of config.dataPath (default ../.beads/issues.jsonl)  2) window.BMC_SNAPSHOT from orchestration-data.js (works on file://)  3) built-in empty/demo state with instructions.
- Authoring: src/ split (engine.mjs pure logic, panel.css, panel.js, panel.template.html) + zero-dependency node build.mjs -> dist/orchestration.html. node --test for gates. NO npm dependencies, node >=18 stdlib only.
- UI language EN by default, every user-visible string overridable via BMC_CONFIG.strings.
- Aesthetic: JARVIS (MCU Tony Stark HUD) - see bmc-3.
- Dogfooding: this repo's own .beads is committed and doubles as the GitHub Pages demo data.

title: Install/wiring scripts ps1+sh: vendor panel into any project, config-preserving updates
description:
CONTEXT: panel is functional (bmc-4) and refresh scripts exist (bmc-5). You own scripts/install.ps1 + scripts/install.sh - the per-project WIRING channel.
BEHAVIOR (identical semantics in both scripts):
- Args: -Target project-root (required), -Dir subdir (default docs), -Update (replace panel + refresh scripts only), -Force (documented as dangerous, see below).
- Fresh install: copy dist/orchestration.html -> target/dir/orchestration.html; copy scripts/refresh.ps1 + refresh.sh -> target/scripts/ (create dirs); generate target/dir/orchestration.config.js stub ONLY IF ABSENT: window.BMC_CONFIG = {title: basename of target + " mission control", dataPath: "../.beads/issues.jsonl"} plus commented-out examples for accent, strings (Italian example), refreshInterval, metaPath.
- NEVER overwrite an existing orchestration.config.js or orchestration.meta.json (not even with -Force; -Force only allows replacing a locally MODIFIED panel file). -Update replaces panel + refresh scripts, touches nothing else.
- Print a JARVIS-flavored next-steps summary: how to run refresh, how to serve live, where config lives, installed panel version (read the version stamp from the panel file).
- Same stdlib constraints as bmc-5 (PowerShell 5.1 + POSIX sh). No network in v1 (install runs from a clone of this repo; the curl one-liner alternative is documented in README by bmc-9, not implemented here).
GATE (scripted E2E as test/install.test.mjs or a node gate script): create a scratch temp project containing a fake .beads/issues.jsonl -> run install.ps1 -> assert files exist + config stub contains the target basename; modify the config file, rerun plain install -> config byte-identical; touch dist/orchestration.html then run -Update -> panel replaced, config untouched. Repeat the key assertions for install.sh via sh. Exit 0.
SECURITY: writes into user projects -> path traversal safe (resolve and verify every write lands inside -Target), refuse to write outside -Target, never delete anything. MANDATORY adversarial review (protocol 0.10).
DO NOT TOUCH: src/, build.mjs.

title: Refresh scripts ps1+sh: .beads -> orchestration-data.js snapshot + bd memories enrichment
description:
CONTEXT: independent of UI work. You own scripts/refresh.ps1 and scripts/refresh.sh.
TASK: generate the offline snapshot file the panel uses on file://.
BEHAVIOR (identical across both scripts):
- Locate .beads/issues.jsonl: explicit -BeadsDir/--beads-dir arg, else walk up from cwd until found; error message with usage if not found.
- Output orchestration-data.js (arg -Out/--out, default ./orchestration-data.js): a JS file assigning window.BMC_SNAPSHOT = {generated_at: ISO8601, source: abs path, issues: [ ...parsed lines passthrough... ]}; also window.BMC_META = passthrough of an optional orchestration.meta.json sibling if present.
- Enrichment (best-effort, NEVER fail the run): if bd is on PATH, capture bd memories relevant to orchestration (keys orchestrator-lock, handoff, attempts/*) into snapshot.orchestrator as raw text values; wrap in try/catch equivalent, skip silently on any error.
- Pure stdlib: PowerShell 5.1-compatible (no ternary, no pipeline-chain operators, -Encoding utf8 explicit, no pwsh-7-only syntax) and POSIX-sh-compatible (no bashisms; jq NOT assumed - passthrough JSONL lines as-is into a JS array, they are already JSON).
- Output must be UTF-8 without BOM and valid JS (the JSONL lines joined with commas inside [ ]). Corrupt/blank lines: skip, count, report count to stderr, still succeed.
- Idempotent, safe to run from a bd hook or cron; exit 0 on success, 1 on no-beads-found.
GATE (run both): powershell -File scripts/refresh.ps1 with out to a temp file against THIS repo's real .beads AND sh scripts/refresh.sh same -> exit 0 each; then a node one-liner loads each output as text, extracts the object literal after the = sign, JSON-parses it, asserts issues.length>0 and generated_at present; outputs of ps1 and sh are semantically equivalent (same issue count). Scripting this gate as test/refresh.test.mjs is encouraged.
SECURITY: these scripts run inside users own projects -> no network, no eval of repo content, never print contents of anything except .beads exports, never write outside the requested out path. This bead gets mandatory adversarial review (protocol 0.10).
DO NOT TOUCH: src/, build.mjs.

title: JARVIS UI: Stark-HUD theme, arc-reactor ring, hook contract, dual theme, i18n hooks
description:
CONTEXT: scaffold exists (bmc-1). You own ONLY src/panel.template.html and src/panel.css. Build the JARVIS/Stark-HUD visual identity. NO JavaScript logic (bmc-4 wires behavior) - but the markup must expose the full hook contract below.
AESTHETIC BRIEF (Marco: interfaccia di JARVIS di Tony Stark, MCU):
- Deep space-navy/near-black bg with subtle hex/grid pattern + faint scanlines (CSS only, no images).
- Holographic cyan as primary luminous ink (glows via text-shadow/box-shadow), gold/amber as secondary accent (alerts, in-progress), desaturated slate for muted.
- Progress ring = ARC REACTOR: layered conic-gradient ring + inner glow + thin rotating tick ring (CSS animation, slow), percentage in the core.
- Cards: thin 1px luminous strokes, HUD corner brackets (::before/::after), status as small uppercase telemetry pills, monospace for ids/data readouts.
- Wave headers as HUD section rules with angular cut (clip-path) numbered badges.
- Boot/scan animation on first paint (staggered fade/slide, under 600ms total). MUST respect prefers-reduced-motion (disable all deco animation).
- Light theme: daylight hologram variant (pale bg, cyan -> deep teal ink) via @media (prefers-color-scheme) AND [data-theme="light"]/[data-theme="dark"] overrides (toggle wins in both directions).
- Everything themable from :root custom props; accent overridable at runtime via BMC_CONFIG.accent (document which single prop to set: --bmc-accent).
HOOK CONTRACT (document in a comment block at top of template; bmc-4 depends on it):
ids: #ring #ringpct #chips #c-all #c-ready #c-prog #c-blocked #c-done #c-deferred #toolbar #search #refresh #auto #src #srctxt #updtxt #waves #foot #toast #themetoggle ; data attrs: .chip[data-f], .id[data-id] ; card classes: .card.{ready|inprogress|blocked|done|deferred} .stpill.* .think.{high|medhi|med|low} .tag.trk .tag.file .warn .blk .who ; template provides <template id="card-tpl"> with one parametric card markup plus a visible dummy grid (one example card per state) that bmc-4 will replace at runtime.
ALL visible strings in the template get data-i18n="key" attributes (EN text inline as default) - list the keys in the contract comment.
NO branding of any project (no ScuolA+, no Orizzonte); panel title placeholder data-i18n="title" default "MISSION CONTROL".
GATE: node build.mjs exits 0 AND grep gates all pass: zero matches for ScuolA+ or Orizzonte; at least 1 match each for prefers-color-scheme, prefers-reduced-motion, data-theme, --bmc-accent, data-i18n. Final visual acceptance = orchestrator review (aesthetic, no fake gate).
DO NOT TOUCH: src/engine.mjs, src/panel.js, build.mjs, test/ (except you MAY add test/theme-contract.test.mjs implementing the grep gates in node).

title: README EN+IT: quickstart, config/overlay reference, ledger doc, FAQ + path-check gate
description:
CONTEXT: everything ships (bmc-7 install, bmc-8 demo). Write the public face. You own README.md + any docs/*.md.
README.md (EN, concise, lightly JARVIS-flavored but professional):
- Hero: one-paragraph pitch + screenshot reference ![panel](docs/screenshot.png) (the orchestrator captures the real image at QA; treat as allowed-missing in the gate) + link to the live Pages demo URL (https://maisdesign.github.io/bd-mission-control/).
- Quickstart, 2 paths: A) one-liner vendoring via curl/iwr of the raw dist/orchestration.html URL + manual 3-line config stub; B) clone + scripts/install.ps1|sh wiring walkthrough.
- How data flows: live fetch -> snapshot (refresh scripts) -> demo fallback; the file:// story explained honestly (browsers block fetch, snapshot solves it).
- Config reference table: every BMC_CONFIG key with type and default (title, accent, dataPath, refreshInterval, strings, metaPath) - VERIFY each against src/panel.js before documenting; do not invent flags.
- Overlay reference: orchestration.meta.json shape with a realistic example (waves/beads/flags) and the philosophy line: curation enriches, never required.
- Verification ledger section: the VERIFIED result=pass comment convention, why closed does not equal verified, generic pointer to multi-agent orchestration workflows (no private machine paths).
- Requirements (any bd version exporting issues.jsonl; node needed only for developing the panel, NOT for using it), Compatibility (PowerShell 5.1+, POSIX sh), FAQ (blank page on file:// -> run refresh; CORS; bd auto-export hint), Contributing (zero-dependency policy is law), License MIT.
- Short "Italiano" section at the bottom: 5-line summary + quickstart in Italian.
GATE: add test/readme.test.mjs: every relative path referenced in README links/images exists in the repo (parse markdown, skip http(s) links, allow-list docs/screenshot.png); required section headings present (Quickstart, Config, FAQ, License). node --test exit 0.
DO NOT TOUCH: src/, scripts/, dist/.

title: GitHub Pages demo: /docs self-contained panel fed by this repo's own beads (dogfood)
description:
CONTEXT: panel works (bmc-4), refresh exists (bmc-5). Build the public demo: GitHub Pages will serve /docs on main.
TASK:
- docs/index.html: the built panel (copied from dist at build time - extend build.mjs with a demo step or add scripts/build-demo.mjs, zero-dep) + docs/orchestration.config.js (title "bd-mission-control // live demo", a short strings override showcasing the i18n feature, accent left default) + docs/orchestration-data.js GENERATED from THIS repo's real .beads via the bmc-5 refresh script (dogfooding: visitors see the actual development tracker of the panel itself, verification ledger included).
- Demo must be fully static and self-contained: no external requests of any kind (fonts/CDN/analytics forbidden); works when served from a subpath (relative paths only).
- Add a footer note via demo config strings: demo data = this repo's own bead tracker.
- Document in the build output how to regenerate the demo (one command).
GATE: demo build command exits 0; docs/index.html + docs/orchestration.config.js + docs/orchestration-data.js exist; node check: demo data parses and issues.length>0; grep gate: zero occurrences of http:// or https:// inside src= or href= attributes in docs/index.html (self-contained).
NOTE: Pages activation itself is done by the orchestrator via gh api after merge - out of your scope.
DO NOT TOUCH: src/panel.*, src/engine.mjs (consume dist output only), scripts/refresh.* (invoke, do not edit).

title: Ledger + orchestrator HUD: VERIFIED badges, drift filter, lock/handoff/attempts strip
description:
CONTEXT: bmc-4 (behaviors) + bmc-5 (snapshot enrichment) landed. Surface orchestration-protocol state in the HUD. You own the ledger/HUD additions in src/panel.js plus minimal additions to src/panel.css under a clearly-marked bmc-6 comment section.
FEATURES:
- Verification badge on done cards: engine already computes verification pass/fail/null. pass -> small cyan check VERIFIED; fail -> amber warning VERIFY FAILED; null on a closed bead -> subtle hollow marker unverified (title tooltip: closed without independent verification - drift per protocol 0.12). Add a toggle or chip "unverified only" to hunt drift quickly.
- Orchestrator HUD strip (collapsible, above #waves): reads snapshot.orchestrator if present: lock holder + last confirmation age (green under 30min, amber under 2h, red older), pending handoff_id if any, attempts/<bead> counts rendered on matching cards as ATT n/3. Entire strip hidden when data absent (live mode without enrichment) - zero visual cost.
- Empty/degraded states must remain elegant (JARVIS flavor: NO TELEMETRY).
TESTS: helpers (age bucketing, ledger-to-badge mapping, attempts parsing) in node --test (test/hud.test.mjs).
GATE: node --test && node build.mjs -> exit 0; orchestrator runtime QA with a snapshot generated by scripts/refresh.ps1 from THIS repo (which has real lock+attempts memories).
DO NOT TOUCH: engine public API (consume snapshot fields only), template hook contract (additive changes only).
